commands.review_summary:
The PR projects the gateway active-run registry into transcript `session.message` snapshots and adds gateway/UI regression tests so WebChat keeps a local run during delayed plugin finalization.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +125. Total +146 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Partly: source inspection shows current main can send a terminal persisted session row through `session.message` without active-run projection, and the UI clears local run state when `hasActiveRun` is not true. I did not establish a live browser/plugin-hook reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because there is no concrete code finding; the external PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof or maintainer override before merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve active runs during plugin finalization
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin `before_agent_finalize` hooks run after the assistant transcript can be persisted but before terminal delivery completes. During that hook-controlled interval, the transcript broadcaster built `session.message` snapshots only from the persisted session row. A row already marked `done` could therefore make WebChat reconcile the local run before the delayed chat terminal arrived, allowing history reconciliation and terminal delivery to race.

This change keeps the fix at the plugin/gateway lifecycle boundary:

- project the gateway's existing Control UI-visible active-run registry into transcript snapshots
- include `hasActiveRun` in both the top-level payload and nested `session` object consumed by WebChat
- leave UI runtime reconciliation, message dedupe, terminal ownership, and history behavior unchanged

## Plugin hook behavior

While a `before_agent_finalize` hook is awaiting, the chat abort-controller entry remains active. A transcript update emitted in that interval now reports `hasActiveRun: true`, even if the persisted row already says `status: "done"`. WebChat therefore keeps the current run until normal terminal delivery.

After terminal lifecycle handling clears `projectSessionActive`, subsequent transcript snapshots report `hasActiveRun: false`.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-session-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts`
  - gateway: 72 tests passed
  - UI: 107 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-session-events.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-subscriptions.ts src/gateway/server-session-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

Autoreview found one P1 during development: the first version projected `hasActiveRun` only at the payload top level, while WebChat consumes the nested `session` object. The patch and consumer regression test were updated accordingly. Follow-up autoreview retries were blocked by the Codex usage limit.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat can prematurely reconcile a run while a plugin `before_agent_finalize` hook delays terminal delivery.

Real environment tested: Focused gateway and Control UI Vitest suites in the OpenClaw source checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-session-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Transcript snapshots contain nested and top-level `hasActiveRun: true` while active projection remains registered; the actual WebChat session consumer preserves `chatRunId`. Once active projection is cleared, snapshots contain `hasActiveRun: false`.

Observed result after fix: All 179 focused regression tests pass, including existing stale-run recovery coverage.

What was not tested: No packaged browser E2E or live third-party plugin installation was run.
